commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Beasts. Hebrew tsiim, "fishermen. "
Serpents. Hebrew ochim. Septuagint, "echo "(Haydock) or "reeds. "Babylon was built on a marshy situation, and Cyrus having let out the waters of the Euphrates, they could never be effectually stopped.
Ostriches. Or swans.
Hairy. Goats, chap. xxxiv. 14. (Calmet)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: There are people who are even worse than wild donkeys, living in the wilderness and kicking. In fact, most of the young people among us are like this. They have wild desires and jump around, kicking and going around unbridled. They spend all their energy on unbecoming behavior. The fathers are to blame. While they hire horse breakers to discipline their horses and do not let the young colt stay untamed for long, they overlook their own young people. The youth are unbridled and have no self-control. They disgrace themselves through their sexual sins, through gambling and through going to the wicked theater.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
